user_query:
I’m prepping for our next editorial meeting and need my recent content organized without taxing your time (pun intended). Using the files under input/, please do the following and save all outputs under outputs/:

1) Inspect files and directories:
- Recursively scan input/opeds/ and input/monologues/ for .txt files. Each file starts with two header lines: "Title: …" and "Tags: …" followed by the body text. Extract the Title, the comma-separated Tags, the Source (opeds or monologues), and the Date from the filename pattern (op-YYYY-MM-DD-*.txt or mono-YYYY-MM-DD-*.txt).
- Normalize tags to canonical topics using input/topics.yaml as a case-insensitive synonym map. Collapse duplicate canonical topics per item (e.g., if both "border" and "immigration" appear on one file, count only one canonical topic: border). For word counts, count only body words (ignore the two header lines) by splitting on whitespace.
- Also load input/quotes.jsonl, normalize its tags using the same topics.yaml, and associate each quote with its canonical topics (de-duplicated per quote).
- Load pun seeds from input/pun_seeds.csv, keyed by canonical topic.

2) Compute basic statistics and aggregates:
- Build outputs/knowledge_summary.csv with one row per canonical topic and columns:
  topic, item_count, oped_count, monologue_count, quote_count, avg_body_word_count
  where item_count is total number of .txt items tagged with the topic (after normalization and per-item de-duplication), oped_count and monologue_count split by source, quote_count is the number of quotes tagged with the topic, and avg_body_word_count is the average number of body words across tagged items, rounded to the nearest integer.
- Build outputs/source_overview.csv with columns: source, item_count, total_words, where total_words is the sum of body word counts for that source.

3) Prepare meeting notes and action items:
- Create outputs/meeting_notes.md with two sections:
  A. Agenda: List the top three canonical topics by item_count from knowledge_summary.csv (break ties alphabetically by topic). For each, include:
     - Topic name and item_count
     - Pun seed (from pun_seeds.csv; if missing, write N/A)
     - Up to two representative items: choose the two most recent items by Date for that topic, and for each show "[YYYY-MM-DD] Source - Title".
  B. Action Items: For each of the top three topics, add bullets:
     - "Review [topic] files: X files" (use item_count)
     - "Pick pun seed: [pun_seed or N/A]"
     - "Select quote: [author] — \"[shortest quote by character length for that topic, truncated to first 80 chars if longer]\""

Notes and constraints:
- Treat tag matching and canonicalization as case-insensitive; trim spaces and punctuation around tags.
- Do not double-count a canonical topic within the same item or quote.
- All outputs should be UTF-8 text files with headers on the CSVs.
- Do not add or remove input files; rely only on the provided inputs.

Deliverables:
- outputs/knowledge_summary.csv
- outputs/source_overview.csv
- outputs/meeting_notes.md
 All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
